title: Pilgrimage and Governors
description: Al-Junayd appointed his officials, giving appointments only to Mudaris. He appointed Qatan b. Qutaybah as governor of Bukhara, al-Walid b. al-Qaga' al-'Absi287 as governor for Herat, Habib b. 281. Or Ibn Khuraym, al-Murri, al-Junayd's first cousin on his father's side. See Crone, Slaves, 98. 282. A town between Samarqand and Bukhara in the Zarafshan Valley seven farsakhs west of Samarqand and thus necessarily close to Kamarjah. See Barthold, Turkestan, 96-7; Le Strange, Lands, 468. 283. Text: fa-usimm; read: fa-usir, as Ms. BM and Cairo ed. 284.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 25
